description_long: This object is a vintage box-style television with a large, slightly curved glass screen set into a thick, black rectangular housing. The front features a prominent bezel framing the screen, with visible wear and scuff marks along the edges, suggesting age and frequent use. Below the screen, there are four rotary control knobs arranged in a horizontal row, likely for power, volume, channel selection, and tuning. The sides and back are solid, flat, and unadorned, with a matte black finish and subtle panel lines. The overall construction appears robust, with the television's body made primarily of metal and plastic, and the screen composed of thick glass. This type of television would commonly be found in urban environments such as old apartments, garages, or curbside for disposal.
description: A vintage, boxy black television with a curved glass screen and four rotary knobs beneath the display, housed in a sturdy rectangular casing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved glass screen, thick bezel, and four rotary control knobs below the display.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the solid, flat black panel with no visible features or openings.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a plain, unadorned black back panel with subtle panel lines.
description_view_3: Right side view, similar to the left, with a flat black surface and no visible controls or ports.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, black, matte plastic bezel with visible wear and scuff marks
attribute_control_button_layout: four rotary knobs in a horizontal row below the screen
